meaning: n.鼻,(飞机、船等的)前端v.侦察出
examples: I wrinkled my nose in disgust.
Aw, she's got her mother's nose!
She punched him on the nose.
When my nose first got a whiff of its pungent, acidic odour, my stomach began to churn.
Kevin Mitnicks crime was to thumb his nose at the costly computer security systems employed by large corporations.
